scope.target: Source-only versus downstream-packaged test applicability contract
constraints: Keep source release and builder tests required in the source repository
Keep applicable target manifest and safe-apply checks packaged
Do not require excluded source release records, tags, or builder modules downstream

steps: Inventory every required command and its packaged dependencies
Classify source-only and downstream-supported tests explicitly
Correct profile, runner projection, and packaged README
Add extracted-package positive and missing-dependency negative fixtures
validation: Source full gate retains release/package-builder coverage
Profile inventory proves every selected command dependency is present
execution.last_completed_step: Excluded source-only release and builder tests from the target payload, gated their source execution, and passed the synchronized extracted-target quick gate.
execution.next_action: Proceed to V041-004 for immutable release-candidate validation and independent verification.

results.summary: Source release and package-builder suites remain required in the source repository, are excluded from the public payload, and are reported as not applicable by the packaged runner and navigation validator. A real v0.3.0 payload upgraded from immutable candidate commit 32212761c8b6e06f895486ca393059645e18d44a and passed all 19 selected downstream quick-gate checks after target-owned synchronization.
tests_run: python .ai/scripts/tests/test_ai_context_package_apply.py -v (13 passed, 1 Windows symlink privilege skip)
results.residual_risk: The target still must run repo-structure-sync or ai-context-upgrader to reconcile target-owned root, catalog, and provenance files; source-only tests are no longer an invalid package dependency.
